title: GitHub Review by gndrmnn
summary: The reviewer asks to remove the `SetCondition` and `GetCondition` wrappers in `conditions.go` and call the upstream `meta.*StatusCondition` functions directly, since the wrappers add no value. Additionally, any helpers that are kept in `conditions.go` should be reused in `internal/common/testutil/assertions` to reduce duplication.

code_comments.body:
Use the upstream API ``meta.*StatusCondition`` functions directly instead of introducing ``SetCondition`` and ``GetCondition`` whose entire purpose is to call them without any clear benefit.

Also, the introduced functions here in ``conditions.go`` should be used in ``internal/common/testutil/assertions`` where it makes sense to reduce duplicated code.
code_comments.author: gndrmnn
code_comments.resolution: Removed `SetCondition` and `GetCondition` wrapper functions entirely. Updated `IsReady` and `AllTrue` to call `meta.FindStatusCondition` directly. Deleted all tests for the removed wrappers and the now-unused `time` import. However, the suggestion to reuse the remaining conditions helpers in `internal/common/testutil/assertions` does not appear to be addressed in this diff.

resolution_summary: Removed the thin wrapper functions `SetCondition` and `GetCondition` from `conditions.go`, replacing all internal call sites with direct calls to the upstream `meta.SetStatusCondition` and `meta.FindStatusCondition` APIs. The corresponding tests for the removed wrappers were also deleted from `conditions_test.go`. The second part of the review (using conditions helpers in `internal/common/testutil/assertions`) is not visibly addressed in this diff.
